The book proceeds in ten parts:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{Part~I} (The Alignment Problem Reframed, Chs.~1--5): Reframes the alignment problem.
\item \textbf{Part~II} (Agents, Boundaries, and Real Optimizers, Chs.~6--10): Develops a non-anthropomorphic account of agents and boundaries.
\item \textbf{Part~III} (Capability Growth and Competence, Chs.~11--14): Treats capability as boundary information and studies growth.
\item \textbf{Part~IV} (Human Values as Needs Smoothed over Time, Chs.~15--19): Introduces value bundles.
\item \textbf{Part~V} (Interpreting a System's Goals, Chs.~20--23): Upgrades goal inference into bundle and bearer inference.
\item \textbf{Part~VI} (Correction Channels, Chs.~24--27): Develops correction channels and extrapolative correction processes.
\item \textbf{Part~VII} (Successors, Reproduction, and Continuity, Chs.~28--31): Studies successors and conserved properties.
\item \textbf{Part~VIII} (Attractor Basins and Socio-Technical Selection, Chs.~32--35): Analyzes socio-technical selection and attractor basins.
\item \textbf{Part~IX} (Safety Cases, Adversaries, and Open Questions, Chs.~36--41): Turns the framework into adversarial measurement, safety cases, and open cruxes.
\item \textbf{Part~X} (The Philosophical and Civilizational Limit, Chs.~42--45): Reaches the philosophical limit: value change, human--AI merger, and what cannot be solved technically.
\end{itemize}
